assertions.assertion: The response describes locating the digest comment using the marker string '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest:' from shared/description-digest-protocol.md
assertions.evidence: Step 1: 'Search through the returned comments for any whose body starts with the marker string [sdlc-workflow] Description digest:, as defined in shared/description-digest-protocol.md'

assertions.assertion: The response describes detecting a mismatch between the stored digest and the computed digest of the current description
assertions.evidence: Step 6: 'MISMATCH DETECTED — The hex digests do not match' with both expected and actual values shown

assertions.assertion: The response alerts the user that the description was modified after plan-feature created the task and displays the expected vs actual digest values
assertions.evidence: Step 7: 'The task description for TC-9201 was modified after plan-feature created it.' Expected: sha256-md:0000... Actual: sha256-md:a1b2...

assertions.assertion: The response offers the user a choice: (1) proceed with the current description, or (2) stop so they can re-run plan-feature
assertions.evidence: Options: '1. Proceed -- continue implementing with the current (modified) description' and '2. Stop -- halt implementation so you can re-run plan-feature'

assertions.assertion: The response stops execution and does not proceed to Step 2 or implementation planning until the user responds — following the same pause-and-ask pattern as the incomplete description handling
assertions.evidence: 'Execution stops here. No subsequent steps are taken -- Step 2 (Verify Dependencies), Step 3 (Transition to In Progress), implementation planning, branching, code changes, and all other downstream work are blocked until the user responds'
